recipes.title: Paella Valenciana
recipes.description: Saffron rice with chicken, rabbit, beans and rosemary, cooked in a wide pan.
steps: Brown the chicken and rabbit in olive oil in a paella pan, then push to the edges.
Sauté the green beans, then add the grated tomato and paprika and cook to a sofrito.
Pour in the hot stock with saffron and bring to a boil, season and add the garrofó beans.
Sprinkle in the rice evenly and do not stir again.
Cook 10 minutes on high heat, then 8 minutes on low to form the socarrat at the base.
Rest with rosemary on top under foil for 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Tortilla Española
recipes.description: Thick Spanish potato and onion omelette, served warm or at room temperature.
steps: Slice the potatoes thinly and confit them with the onion in olive oil over low heat 25 minutes until tender.
Drain, reserving the oil, and season generously.
Whisk the eggs in a large bowl, then fold in the warm potatoes and onion.
Heat 2 tbsp reserved oil in a non-stick pan and add the mixture, cooking 4 minutes on medium heat.
Slide onto a plate, invert back into the pan and cook another 3 minutes for a juicy centre.
Rest 5 minutes, then cut into wedges.

recipes.title: Gazpacho Andaluz
recipes.description: Cold Andalusian raw vegetable soup with tomato, pepper, cucumber and bread.
steps: Soak the bread in a little water until soft.
Roughly chop the tomatoes, cucumber, pepper and garlic.
Blend everything with the bread, vinegar and salt until smooth.
Stream in the olive oil to emulsify the soup.
Strain through a fine sieve and chill at least 2 hours.
Serve very cold with a drizzle of olive oil and finely diced vegetables on top.

recipes.title: Patatas Bravas
recipes.description: Crisp fried potatoes with smoky tomato bravas sauce and aioli.
steps: Cube the potatoes and confit gently in olive oil for 15 minutes until tender.
Increase the heat and fry until golden and crisp; drain on paper.
Sauté garlic in 2 tbsp oil, add both paprikas and the passata; simmer 10 minutes.
Blend the bravas sauce with the vinegar and season.
Whisk crushed garlic into the mayonnaise to make a quick aioli.
Serve the potatoes hot with both sauces drizzled on top.

recipes.title: Albóndigas
recipes.description: Spanish meatballs simmered in a rich tomato and almond sauce.
steps: Soak breadcrumbs in milk, then mix with the meats, egg, garlic, parsley and seasoning.
Roll into walnut-sized meatballs and brown in olive oil until golden.
Sauté the onion in the same pan, then add paprika and passata.
Blend the toasted almonds with a ladle of sauce and stir back in.
Return the meatballs to the pan and simmer 20 minutes until cooked through.
Serve as tapas with bread or with rice for a main.

recipes.title: Croquetas de Jamón
recipes.description: Crisp breaded croquettes with a creamy béchamel and Iberian ham filling.
steps: Sauté the ham in butter, then sift in flour and cook 2 minutes.
Whisk in warm milk gradually to make a thick béchamel; season with nutmeg.
Pour into a tray, cover and chill several hours until firm.
Shape into small cylinders, then coat in flour, beaten egg and breadcrumbs.
Fry in 180°C oil until deep golden and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve hot.

recipes.title: Pulpo a la Gallega
recipes.description: Galician octopus on potato slices with paprika, sea salt and olive oil.
steps: Freeze the octopus for at least a day to tenderise it, then thaw.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil with the bay leaf and dip the octopus three times before submerging.
Simmer 40 minutes until a skewer slides easily into the thickest tentacle.
Boil the potatoes whole until tender, then peel and slice into thick rounds.
Slice the octopus tentacles into bite-sized rounds and arrange over the potatoes.
Sprinkle with paprika and salt and douse generously with olive oil.

recipes.title: Cocido Madrileño
recipes.description: Madrid chickpea stew served in three courses: broth, vegetables, then meats.
steps: Soak the chickpeas overnight in salted water.
Place beef, chicken and ham bone in a large pot of water and simmer, skimming.
After an hour, add the chickpeas (in a net) and carrots and simmer 90 minutes more.
Add the chorizo, morcilla and potatoes and cook another 30 minutes.
Boil the cabbage separately, then sauté in olive oil with garlic.
Strain the broth, cook noodles in it for the soup; serve the broth first, vegetables second, meats third.

recipes.title: Fabada Asturiana
recipes.description: Asturian white bean stew with chorizo, morcilla and pork shoulder.
steps: Soak the beans overnight in cold water.
Place beans, lacón, tocino and onion in a pot, cover with water and bring to a boil.
Skim, lower the heat and simmer gently 90 minutes, adding cold water if needed (called 'asustar').
Add chorizo and morcilla, paprika and saffron and simmer another 30 minutes.
The beans should be creamy and the broth thickened naturally.
Rest the stew, then serve with the meats sliced on top.

recipes.title: Pisto Manchego
recipes.description: La Mancha sautéed vegetable stew with tomato, courgette, pepper and onion.
steps: Dice the onion and peppers and sauté in olive oil 10 minutes until soft.
Add the diced courgette and cook another 8 minutes.
Stir in grated tomatoes, garlic and sugar and simmer 25 minutes.
Season generously and reduce until jammy.
Crack eggs into wells in the pisto and cook until just set, if you wish.
Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Gambas al Ajillo
recipes.description: Sizzling prawns in olive oil with garlic and chili.
steps: Warm the olive oil in a small terracotta dish with the garlic and chili.
Cook gently until the garlic is golden but not burnt.
Add the prawns and turn up the heat to sizzling.
Cook 2 minutes, then add paprika and sherry and toss.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately with bread to soak up the oil.

recipes.title: Churros
recipes.description: Fried choux sticks dusted with sugar, dunked in thick hot chocolate.
steps: Boil the water with a pinch of salt, tip in the flour and stir to a smooth paste.
Spoon into a piping bag fitted with a star tip.
Pipe lengths of dough into 180°C oil and snip with scissors.
Fry until golden and crisp, then drain and roll in sugar.
Warm the milk, whisk in cornflour and pour over chopped chocolate to make a thick sauce.
Serve the churros hot with the chocolate for dipping.

recipes.title: Pan con Tomate
recipes.description: Catalan toasted bread rubbed with garlic and tomato, drizzled with olive oil.
steps: Toast the bread until crisp on both sides.
Halve the garlic and rub the cut side over the warm bread.
Halve the tomatoes and rub them over the bread until the flesh is gone and the seeds soak in.
Drizzle generously with olive oil.
Sprinkle with flaky salt and eat immediately.

recipes.title: Salmorejo
recipes.description: Cordoban thick chilled tomato cream topped with egg and Iberian ham.
steps: Blend tomatoes, bread, garlic and salt until smooth.
Stream in the olive oil and vinegar to emulsify into a thick orange cream.
Strain to remove tomato skins.
Chill at least 2 hours before serving.
Pour into bowls and top with chopped boiled egg and torn Iberian ham.

recipes.title: Pollo al Ajillo
recipes.description: Andalusian chicken sautéed with whole garlic cloves, sherry and bay.
steps: Brown the seasoned chicken in olive oil, skin side first, until golden.
Add the whole garlic cloves and bay leaves and sauté until fragrant.
Pour in the sherry and let it bubble for a minute.
Cover and simmer 25 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Sprinkle with thyme and serve with bread to mop up the garlicky juices.

recipes.title: Crema Catalana
recipes.description: Catalan citrus and cinnamon custard with a brittle burnt-sugar crust.
steps: Infuse the milk with cinnamon and lemon zest over low heat for 10 minutes.
Whisk yolks with sugar and cornflour until pale.
Strain the warm milk over the yolk mixture, whisking constantly.
Return to the pan and cook gently until thickened.
Pour into shallow ramekins and chill at least 2 hours.
Sprinkle with demerara sugar and caramelise with a torch to a glassy crust.
